name: Khalaj

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Khalaj Turkic (turk1303).

notes: Skeleton. Khalaj is a divergent Turkic language of central Iran, here written in a Latin orthography of the Azerbaijani/Turkish type. Consonant and vowel letters are given their conventional Turkic-Latin values, including c (dʒ), ç (tʃ), ş (ʃ), j (ʒ), x (x), ğ (ɣ), q (uvular/back stop), the front-rounded ö and ü, back unrounded ı, and ə. Khalaj is notable for preserving Proto-Turkic word-initial *h and for a phonemic long/short vowel contrast; vowel length is not marked in this map and is left unencoded, and the exact digraph conventions of the source orthography may vary, so a moderate PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Khalaj Turkic (turk1303)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
